fields.description:
## Context
rebar records NO session identifier on a claim distinct from `assignee` and the event
`author`. A claim writes a `STATUS(open->in_progress)` (+ `EDIT(assignee)`); the envelope
carries `timestamp`, `uuid`, `event_type`, `author` (git user), and `env_id` ‚Äî but
`env_id` is the tracker init-environment id (written once at `rebar init`, shared by ALL
events), NOT a per-session/per-agent id. So an `in_progress` state cannot be attributed to
the specific agent **session** that claimed it.

The design for this epic is complete (unknowns resolved, experiments run, OSS surveyed).
Full design doc lives with the driving session; the resolved decisions are below.

## What
When a coding-agent session id is present in the environment, automatically record it on
the `open->in_progress` transition so an in_progress ticket is attributable to the session
that started it ‚Äî additively, without disturbing `assignee`/`author`, local-only,
compaction-safe, tolerated by older clones.

## Design decisions (resolved)
- **Env-var contract (ordered, data-driven list).** Explicit rebar var first, then native
  harness vars by popularity, ambient last: `REBAR_SESSION_ID` -> `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`
  -> `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` -> `SESSION_ID` -> None. (`CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID` captured
  as a secondary `remote_session` field.) **Design correction (story 7656):** Codex is NOT a
  native var in this list ‚Äî it exposes no supported readable session env var, so it is covered
  by its SessionStart shim exporting `REBAR_SESSION_ID` instead (there is no `CODEX_THREAD_ID`).
  Never fall back to git HEAD (changes per commit). One shared resolver replaces today's two
  divergent ones.
- **Storage.** Record on the claim `STATUS` event's `data["session"]`; fold to reducer
  state key `claimed_session` (mirrors `assignee`). No new event type, no envelope change.
- **Jira.** N/A ‚Äî a local reducer-state key never enters the reconciler (outbound differ
  is an explicit allow-list).
- **Reliable capture.** `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` is an env var only in the remote runner;
  the local CLI needs a SessionStart hook that exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID`.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] A claim made while a recognized session env var is set durably records that session
      id, queryable later, distinct from assignee/author.
- [ ] Absent the env var, behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged (fully backward compatible).
- [ ] The recorded id survives compaction and is preserved-and-ignored by older clones.
- [ ] Works in the LOCAL Claude Code CLI (not only the remote runner), via a capture shim.
- [ ] Session id is attributable across the popular OSS harnesses (Claude Code, OpenCode,
      Codex, Cursor) through a portable contract.

## Context (gotchas to respect ‚Äî from OSS prior art)
- Session ids are NOT stable across `--resume`/`--continue`/fork/subagents; treat the
  record as "the session that emitted this claim event", not a per-ticket lifetime id.
- A leaked/stale env var pollutes unrelated sessions (rebar bug 76e9 class) ‚Äî prefer
  hook-injected, session-scoped values.
- Keep it local-only + opaque (consent/provenance sensitivity); read the var as a plain
  string, never interpolate/execute it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Decomposed into discrete-deliverable child stories parented to THIS epic (via each
      child's `parent_id`, confirmable with `rebar deps 38ec-5f12-fdf5-4340`): S1
      `6014-0863-2e36-41b3`, S2 `68ef-da4f-b64b-4903`, S3 `199b-f56f-5ba1-468b`, S5
      `c557-8f46-0c16-4bf5`, S4 `ec5c-bd06-aa49-42d0`, S6 `7656-0a67-e881-4131`, plus the
      module-size split refactor `458f-5b67-961e-4e80` ‚Äî all closed.
- [x] Env-var contract implemented as the ordered, extensible resolver above
      (`REBAR_SESSION_ID -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> OPENCODE_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID`, with
      Codex covered by its `REBAR_SESSION_ID` shim rather than a native `CODEX_THREAD_ID` ‚Äî see
      `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py`).
- [x] Storage shape implemented (STATUS `data["session"]` -> `claimed_session` state).
- [x] Claim/transition records the id when present; no-op when absent.
- [x] Read surface exposes the claiming session id for a ticket.
- [x] Backward/forward compatible + compaction-safe (regression-tested).
- [x] Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims.
